forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system east of Tasmania will maintain a ridge over SA this week. A cold front is expected to weaken and slide across western and southern coasts on Wednesday. A second cold front may pass over the south again on Saturday with a high pressure system to follow over waters south of the Bight on Sunday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Far West Coast: WA-SA Border to Fowlers Bay

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Tuesday for Far West Coast
forecast-period.text: Northerly 15 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots offshore in the late morning. Winds shifting southerly in the evening.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon, then increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres later in the evening.
Southwesterly 1.5 metres inshore, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres offshore.
Partly cloudy. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.
South to southeasterly 15 to 20 knots turning easterly during the day.
Easterly 15 to 20 knots turning north to northeasterly during the morning.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres offshore.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1.5 metres during the evening.
